c.d: Number of active users on the platform. A larger user base increases the likelihood of sales.
Listing fees, commission fees, or transaction fees charged by the app. Lower fees increase seller profit.
How easy it is to list items, manage inventory, and communicate with buyers.
Availability of prepaid shipping labels, integrated shipping options, and buyer-paid shipping.
Variety of payment methods accepted and ease of transferring funds to the seller.
Specific types of items or categories that the app is best suited for (e.g., fashion, electronics, handmade items).
The geographical reach of the platform, whether it's local, national, or international.
Availability of tools like analytics, listing templates, and automated listing renewals.
Ability to list items on multiple platforms simultaneously to increase visibility.
Whether the platform specializes in certain categories like luxury items, vintage goods, or electronics.
